briefdescription.para: Mbox write register.
Mbox read register.
Mbox status reg.
Mbox Error reg.
Mbox send interrupt threshold register.
Mbox receive interrupt threshold register.
Mbox interrupt status register.
Mbox interrupt enable register.
Mbox interrupt pending register.
Mbox control register.

description.para: These status bits are used to poll the FIFOs

briefdescription.para: Receive FIFO is Empty.
Send FIFO is Full.
Send FIFO Threshold Status.
Receive FIFO Threshold Status.

description.para: The IS, IE, and IP registers all have the same bit definition.

briefdescription.para: Send Threshold Active, when the number of Send FIFO entries is less than and equal to Send Interrupt Threshold.
Receive Threshold Active, when the number of Receive FIFO entries is greater than Receive Interrupt Threshold.
Mailbox Error, when read on empty or write on full.
Receive FIFO is Empty.
Send FIFO is Full.
Clear Send FIFO.
Clear Receive FIFO.
Initializes a specific mailbox.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMbox instance to be worked on.
is the device configuration structure containing required HW build data.
is the Physical address of the hardware in a Virtual Memory operating system environment. It is the Base Address in a stand alone environment.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Reads requested bytes from the mailbox referenced by InstancePtr,into the buffer pointed to by the provided pointer.

detaileddescription.para: The number of bytes must be a multiple of 4 (bytes). If not, the call will fail in an assert.
This function is non blocking.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XMbox instance to be worked on.
BufferPtr
is the buffer to read the mailbox contents into, aligned to a word boundary.
RequestedBytes
is the number of bytes of data requested.
BytesRecvdPtr
is the memory that is updated with the number of bytes of data actually read.
XST_SUCCESS on success.
XST_NO_DATA ifthere was no data in the mailbox.
On success, the number of bytes read is returned through the pointer. The call may return with fewer bytes placed in the buffer than requested (not including zero). This is not necessarily an error condition and indicates the amount of data that was currently available in the mailbox.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Reads requested bytes from the mailbox referenced by InstancePtr,into the buffer pointed to by the provided pointer.

detaileddescription.para: The number of bytes must be a multiple of 4 (bytes). If not, the call will fail in an assert.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMbox instance to be worked on.
is the buffer to read the mailbox contents into, aligned to a word boundary.
is the number of bytes of data requested.

simplesect.para: The call blocks until the number of bytes requested are available.

briefdescription.para: Writes the requested bytes from the buffer pointed to by the provided pointer into the mailbox referenced by InstancePtr.The number of bytes must be a multiple of 4 (bytes).

detaileddescription.para: If not, the call will fail in an assert.
This function is non blocking.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XMbox instance to be worked on.
BufferPtr
is the source data buffer, aligned to a word boundary.
RequestedBytes
is the number of bytes requested to be written.
BytesSentPtr
points to memory which is updated with the actual number of bytes written, return value.
- XST_SUCCESS on success. - XST_FIFO_NO_ROOM if the fifo was full.
On success, the number of bytes successfully written into the destination mailbox is returned in the provided pointer. The call may return with zero. This is not necessarily an error condition and indicates that the mailbox is currently full.

simplesect.para: The provided buffer pointed to by BufferPtr must be aligned to a word boundary.

briefdescription.para: Writes the requested bytes from the buffer pointed to by the provided pointer into the mailbox referenced by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: The number of bytes must be a multiple of 4 (bytes). If not, the call will fail in an assert.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMbox instance to be worked on.
is the source data buffer, aligned to a word boundary.
is the number of bytes requested to be written.

simplesect.para: The call blocks until the number of bytes requested are written. The provided buffer pointed to by BufferPtr must be aligned to a word boundary.

briefdescription.para: Checks to see if there is data available to be read.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMbox instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: FALSE if there is data to be read.
TRUE is there no data to be read.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Checks to see if there is room in the write FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMbox instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: FALSE if there is room in write FIFO.
TRUE if there is room in write FIFO.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Resets the mailbox FIFOs by emptying the READ FIFO and making sure the Error Status is zero.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMbox instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if there are any outstanding errors.

simplesect.para: Data from read FIFO is thrown away.

briefdescription.para: Resets the mailbox FIFOs by clearing the READ and WRITE FIFOs using the hardware control register for memory mapped IO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMbox instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Use XMbox_Flush instead for FSL based access.

briefdescription.para: Sets the interrupt enable register for this mailbox.

detaileddescription.para: This function can only be used for Non-FSL interface. If not, the function will fail in an assert.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is a logical OR of XMB_IX_* constants found in xmbox_hw.h.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the interrupt enable for the mailbox.

detaileddescription.para: AND the result of this function with XMB_IX_* to determine which interrupts of this mailbox are enabled. This function can only be used for Non-FSL interface. If not, the function will fail in an assert.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Mask of interrupt bits made up of XMB_IX_* constants found in xmbox_hw.h.
None.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the interrupt status for the mailbox.

detaileddescription.para: AND the results of this function with XMB_IX_* to determine which interrupts are currently pending to the processor. This function can only be used for Non-FSL interface. If not, the function will fail in an assert.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Mask of interrupt bits made up of XMB_IX_* constants found in xmbox_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Clears pending interrupts with the provided mask.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called after the software has serviced the interrupts that are pending. This function clears the corresponding bits of the Interrupt Status Register. This function can only be used for Non-FSL interface. If not, the function will fail in an assert.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is a logical OR of XMB_IX_* constants found in xmbox_hw.h.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Sets the Send Interrupt Threshold.

detaileddescription.para: This function can only be used for Non-FSL interface. If not, the function will fail in an assert.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
is a value to set for the SIT. Only lower Log2(FIFO Depth) bits are used.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Set the Receive Interrupt Threshold.

detaileddescription.para:
This function can only be used for Non-FSL interface. If not, the function will fail in an assert.
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
Value
is a value to set for the RIT. Only lower Log2(FIFO Depth) bits are used.
None.

briefdescription.para: Returns Status register contents.

detaileddescription.para:
This function can only be used for Non-FSL interface. If not, the function will fail in an assert.
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the instance to be worked on.
Value returns Status Register contents.
None.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XMbox_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xmbox.h for the definition of XMbox_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Read one of the mbox registers.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the mbox device.
contains the offset from the 1st register of the mbox to select the specific register of the mbox.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register, a 32 bit value.
C-style signature: u32 XMbox_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, unsigned RegOffset);

briefdescription.para: Write a specified value to a register of a mbox.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the mbox device.
contain the offset from the 1st register of the mbox to select the specific register of the mbox.
is the 32 bit value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: None
C-style signature: void XMbox_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, unsigned RegOffset, u32 ValueToWrite);

briefdescription.para: Write the mbox write register.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the mbox device.
contains the value to be written.

simplesect.para: None.
C-style signature: u32 XMbox_WriteMBox(u32 BaseAddress, u32 ValueToWrite);

briefdescription.para: Read the mbox read FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the mbox device.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register, a 32 bit value.
C-style signature: u32 XMbox_ReadMBox(u32 BaseAddress);

briefdescription.para: Checks if the Read FIFO is Empty.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the mbox device.

listitem.para: TRUE if the FIFO is empty.
FALSE if there is data to be read from the FIFO.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XMbox_IsEmptyHw(u32 BaseAddress);

briefdescription.para: Checks if there is room in the Write FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the mbox device.

listitem.para: FALSE if there is room in FIFO,
TRUE if there is no room in the FIFO

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XMbox_IsFullHw(u32 BaseAddress);

briefdescription.para: Stub implementation of XMbox_FSLReadMBox for non-MicroBlaze platforms.

parameterdescription.para: is unused.

simplesect.para: Always returns 0.
This is an empty stub to allow compilation on non-MicroBlaze platforms. FSL access is only available on MicroBlaze.

briefdescription.para: Stub implementation of XMbox_FSLWriteMBox for non-MicroBlaze platforms.

parameterdescription.para: is unused.
is unused.

simplesect.para: None.
This is an empty stub to allow compilation on non-MicroBlaze platforms. FSL access is only available on MicroBlaze.

briefdescription.para: Stub implementation of XMbox_FSLIsEmpty for non-MicroBlaze platforms.

parameterdescription.para: is unused.

simplesect.para: Always returns 0.
This is an empty stub to allow compilation on non-MicroBlaze platforms. FSL access is only available on MicroBlaze.

briefdescription.para: Stub implementation of XMbox_FSLIsFull for non-MicroBlaze platforms.

parameterdescription.para: is unused.

simplesect.para: Always returns 0.
This is an empty stub to allow compilation on non-MicroBlaze platforms. FSL access is only available on MicroBlaze.

briefdescription.para: Contains required functions for the XMbox driver.

detaileddescription.para: Implements static initialization See xmbox.h for more information about the component.
This header file contains identifiers and driver macros that can be used to access the device.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of Mbox devices in the system.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a va First release 1.00a ecm 06/01/07 Cleanup, new coding standard, check into XCS 1.01a ecm 08/19/08 Fixed the receive routine, FSL leg, was using SendID instead of the correct RecvID. Fixed CRs 466320, 466322, 476535, 476242, 476243 new rev 2.00a hm 04/09/09 Added support for mailbox v2.0, which has interrupts; Fixed CR 502464, which removed extra definitions that are not associated with the interface. Fixed the canonical definition so that each interface is considered as a device instance. 3.00a hbm 10/19/09 Migrated to HAL phase 1 to use xil_io. Removed _m from the function names. Renamed _mIsEmpty to _IsEmptyHw and _mIsFull to _IsFullHw. 3.02a bss 08/18/12 Added XMbox_GetStatus API for CR 676187 4.1 sk 11/10/15 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress CR# 867425. Changed the prototypes of XMbox_CfgInitialize API. 4.3 sa 04/20/17 Support for FIFO reset using hardware control register. 4.10 ht 02/24/26 Fix doxygen warnings
Ver Who Date Changes ----- ---- -------- --------------------------------------------------- 1.00a ecm 06/01/07 Cleanup, new coding standard, check into XCS

detaileddescription.para: None.
The user should refer to the hardware device specification for more details of the device operation. The driver functions/APIs are defined in xmbox.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a va First release 1.00a ecm 06/01/07 Cleanup, new coding standard, check into XCS 1.00a ecm 08/19/08 Fixed CRs 466320, 466322, 476535, 476242, 476243 new rev 2.00a hm 04/09/09 Added support for mailbox v2.0, which has interrupts. Fixed CR 502464, which removed extra definitions that are not associated with the interface. Fixed the canonical definition so that each interface is considered as a device instance. 3.00a hbm 10/19/09 Migrated to HAL phase 1 to use xil_io. Removed _m from the function names. Renamed _mIsEmpty to _IsEmptyHw and _mIsFull to _IsFullHw. 4.3 sa 04/20/17 Added CTRL register definitions. 4.10 ht 02/24/26 Fix doxygen warnings

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm 06/01/07 Cleanup, new coding standard, check into XCS 4.2 ms 08/07/17 Fixed compilation warnings. 4.6 ht 07/06/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 4.10 vlt 12/14/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details. 4.10 ht 02/24/26 Fix doxygen warnings
